decisions.title: Commit to Explore V2 as default experience by Q2 2026
decisions.context: Multiple Legend V2 components (ENG-8544, 8546-8548), data selector polish (ENG-9017), axes dropzones (ENG-10007), and jump-to-Explorer-2 features (ENG-9809) signal maturity. North Star depends on capturing decisions in modern interface.

arguments.for: Engineering velocity on V2 features high, user adoption tracking positive, legacy tech debt accumulating. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' pillar.
arguments.against: Multi-asset panel issues (ENG-10097, 9818) not fully resolved, could impact power users. May need gradual rollout strategy.

decisions.title: Approve Canvas block metadata architecture for production
decisions.context: Canvas is key 2026 product for 'Embed Deeper' pillar. Metadata 1-pager (ENG-10094) represents foundational architecture decision affecting extensibility.

arguments.for: Unblocks CEL expression features (ENG-9538), enables richer decision capture. Critical for Canvas differentiation vs competitors.
arguments.against: Architecture decisions are hard to reverse. Need validation from 3-5 design partners before committing to metadata schema.

decisions.title: Accelerate FedRAMP compliance timeline for GovCloud expansion
decisions.context: Multiple FedRAMP tickets (ENG-9341 log retention, ENG-10070 encrypted storage, ENG-9662 Amplitude) indicate systematic compliance push. Represents 'Build the Moat' opportunity in regulated sectors.
decisions.blockedBy: Amplitude FedRAMP requirements not fully scoped (ENG-9662). Need legal/compliance approval on log retention policy changes.

arguments.for: GovCloud customers have 3-5x higher LTV, limited competition in compliant analytics space. Early mover advantage in defense/aerospace.
arguments.against: Diverts engineering from core product innovation. Compliance is table stakes, not differentiation. May slow feature velocity 15-20%.

decisions.title: Invest in Python Rules production readiness vs new rule types
decisions.context: No Python Rules tickets in current sprint signals potential deprioritization. Yet it's listed as key 2026 product for 'Embed Deeper' pillar.

arguments.for: Data science teams need Python for complex decision logic. Competitors lack this capability. Directly drives Decisions Captured metric.
arguments.against: Current sprint focused on data import (FD-68, FD-43) and infrastructure. May indicate market feedback shows low demand for Python vs SQL/CEL.

decisions.title: Standardize feature flag failure behavior across all services
decisions.context: ENG-9995 addresses feature flag errors, ENG-10104 removes flags for shipped features. Indicates technical debt in flag management affecting reliability.

arguments.for: Feature flag failures cause production incidents affecting 'Nail the Core' reliability. Cleanup enables faster iteration and reduces cognitive load.
arguments.against: Low user-facing impact. Resources better spent on Canvas/Explore V2. Can address incrementally vs dedicated project.

alerts: 3 one-on-ones today - consider batching for Thursday anchor day

32 meeting hours exceeds healthy limit of 25h/week for leadership role
No product review scheduled - Strategy>Evidence>Decision loop incomplete

timeAudit.weekSummary: Meeting-heavy week (80% calendar) limiting strategic decision throughput. Recommend: decline 3 non-critical syncs, block Friday AM for FedRAMP/Canvas architecture deep work, schedule async product review.
